home.name: Dr. Olivia Mathot
home.description-line1: Dermatologie médicale, esthétique et pédiatrique, avec une approche personnalisée et une attention particulière aux pathologies unguéales (affections des ongles).
home.description-line2: Des soins fondés sur une évaluation approfondie, une communication claire et les connaissances médicales les plus récentes.

about.title: À propos
about.subtitle: Dr Olivia Mathot a effectué
about.description: ses études de médecine à l’Université Libre de Bruxelles (ULB), où elle a obtenu son diplôme de Médecin en 2020. Elle s’est ensuite spécialisée en Dermatologie-Vénéréologie et a obtenu son titre de spécialiste en 2025, tous deux avec la plus grande distinction.

contact.appointmentRedirectBefore: Pour planifier une consultation, utilisez la page

contact.medicalDisclaimerTitle: Conseil médical et protection des données
contact.medicalDisclaimerParagraph1: Conformément au Code de déontologie médicale et à la réglementation RGPD relative à la protection des données de santé, aucun avis médical n’est donné par e‑mail.
contact.medicalDisclaimerParagraph2: Un diagnostic fiable nécessite un examen clinique et un échange complet. Par ailleurs, l’e‑mail ne constitue pas un moyen de communication sécurisé pour les données médicales et ne répond pas au cadre légal de la téléconsultation.
contact.medicalDisclaimerParagraph3: Les prescriptions et attestations sont délivrées lors de la consultation.
contact.medicalDisclaimerParagraph4: Les patients déjà suivis peuvent, si nécessaire, utiliser les coordonnées communiquées lors de la consultation.
contact.medicalDisclaimerParagraph5After: on how your personal data is processed and protected.

practicalInfo.title: Informations pratiques
practicalInfo.intro: Afin de vous accueillir dans les meilleures conditions, nous vous invitons à lire les informations pratiques ci-dessous avant votre rendez-vous.
practicalInfo.section1List1: Les consultations se font uniquement sur rendez-vous.
practicalInfo.section1List2: Prendre rendez-vous : via la page de rendez-vous ou par téléphone.
practicalInfo.section1List3: Veuillez réserver un créneau par personne et enregistrer le rendez-vous au nom du patient. Un seul rendez-vous par jour et par personne peut être réservé.
practicalInfo.section1List4: Pour les problèmes urgents (p. ex. suspicion de cancer de la peau), un rendez-vous rapide peut être organisé sur demande spécifique de votre médecin généraliste via le secrétariat.
practicalInfo.section1List5: En cas d'empêchement, merci de nous prévenir au moins 48 heures à l'avance via le lien de l'e-mail de confirmation ou par téléphone.
practicalInfo.section1List6: Attention : en cas d'absence ou d'annulation tardive, un forfait de 25 EUR sera facturé.
practicalInfo.section2List1: Apportez votre carte d'identité électronique (eID), Kids-ID ou carte ISI+.
practicalInfo.section2List2: Une liste de vos médicaments actuels et de vos allergies connues.
practicalInfo.section2List3: Des informations sur les traitements (crèmes ou médicaments) déjà essayés pour votre problème de peau.
practicalInfo.section2List4: Important pour les problèmes d'ongles : veuillez retirer tout vernis (classique ou semi-permanent) ou faux ongles avant la consultation afin de permettre un examen correct.
practicalInfo.section2List5: Votre lettre de référence si vous êtes adressé(e) par un autre médecin.
practicalInfo.section2List6: Merci de signaler pendant la consultation si vous êtes enceinte ou si vous allaitez.
practicalInfo.section2List7: Les prescriptions et attestations sont délivrées uniquement pendant la consultation.
practicalInfo.section3Paragraph1: Les résultats d'analyses ou de biopsies sont généralement discutés lors d'une consultation de suivi. Si nécessaire, le médecin vous contactera personnellement pour communiquer les résultats ou planifier un nouveau rendez-vous.
practicalInfo.section4List1: Dr. Mathot est accréditée et non conventionnée.
practicalInfo.section4List2: Tarifs de base : 50 EUR (Arsenal Clinic) à 70 EUR (Dermatology & Research Institute).
practicalInfo.section4List3: Le régime du tiers payant est appliqué : si votre assurance est en ordre, son intervention est déduite directement. Vous ne payez que le montant restant (50 EUR ou 70 EUR).
practicalInfo.section4List4: Un supplément peut être facturé selon les actes techniques réalisés (cryothérapie, prélèvement, chirurgie, laser, etc.).
practicalInfo.section4List5: Vous ne devez envoyer aucune attestation à votre mutuelle.
practicalInfo.section4List6: Les frais de laboratoire vous sont facturés directement par le laboratoire.
practicalInfo.section4List7: Le paiement se fait de préférence de manière électronique (Bancontact, Payconiq ou application bancaire).
practicalInfo.section5Paragraph1: Pour vous guider au mieux, vous trouverez ci-dessous une sélection de documents pratiques à consulter avant ou après votre rendez-vous.
practicalInfo.section5Paragraph2: Ces informations sont destinées à vous aider à mieux comprendre votre parcours de soins et à préparer votre consultation de façon optimale.
practicalInfo.section5Paragraph3: Ces documents sont uniquement informatifs et ne remplacent en aucun cas un avis médical personnalisé.
practicalInfo.section5PreparationSummary1: Une bonne préparation permet une consultation plus efficace et une évaluation plus précise de votre situation.
practicalInfo.section5PreparationSummary2: Vous trouverez les consignes détaillées de préparation par type de consultation dans les documents ci-dessous.
practicalInfo.section5PreparationLink: Voir les documents de préparation ci-dessous.
practicalInfo.section5SkinCancerIntro: Le cancer de la peau est la forme de cancer la plus fréquente. Un dépistage précoce est essentiel pour un traitement efficace. Via les liens ci-dessous, proposés par la campagne européenne EUROMELANOMA, vous trouverez des informations fiables sur les types de peau, les facteurs de risque, les types de cancers cutanés et la protection contre les UV.
practicalInfo.section5List10: Types de cancers de la peau
practicalInfo.section5List12: Exposition solaire, bronzage et bancs solaires
practicalInfo.section5Paragraph13: Voir le document 'Préparer votre consultation esthétique'.
practicalInfo.section5Paragraph15: Voir le document 'Préparer votre consultation hyperpigmentation'.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticIntro2: Each face is unique, which is why a personalized assessment is essential. This approach is primarily medical: it is based on a precise anatomical analysis and current scientific evidence to propose a tailored, gradual and natural treatment plan.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticExpectations1: What concerns you most? (wrinkles, spots, skin laxity, texture, dark circles, scars, etc.)
practicalInfo.section5AestheticExpectations2: What type of result are you aiming for? (a refreshed look, subtle outcome, overall improvement)
practicalInfo.section5AestheticHistory1: Have you previously undergone aesthetic treatments? (injections, laser, peels, surgery, etc.)
practicalInfo.section5AestheticHistory2: If so: treated areas, dates, types of products used, and results obtained.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticMedical1: Chronic or autoimmune diseases, or coagulation disorders?
practicalInfo.section5AestheticMedical2: History of herpes or abnormal scarring (keloids)?
practicalInfo.section5AestheticMedical3: Current treatments: medications (anticoagulants, aspirin, anti-inflammatory drugs), supplements, or active dermatological treatments (retinoids, acids).
practicalInfo.section5AestheticMedical4: Pregnancy and breastfeeding: as a precaution, most aesthetic procedures are contraindicated during this period. Please inform me if applicable.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticProcess1: An aesthetic consultation is a full medical act that follows a structured and regulated process:
practicalInfo.section5AestheticProcess2: Comprehensive assessment: evaluation of your skin and discussion of available treatment options (topical treatments, peels, lasers, injections...).
practicalInfo.section5AestheticProcess3: Informed information: expected benefits, as well as limitations, risks and potential side effects of each technique will be explained.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticProcess4: Quotation and informed consent: following the consultation, you will receive a detailed quotation and an informed consent form.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticProcess5: Cooling-off period: in accordance with medical ethics, a reflection period (usually 15 days) is respected between the quotation and the procedure.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticNature1: Aesthetic treatments are medical procedures that require appropriate indication.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticNature2: The physician reserves the right to refuse a treatment if it presents a risk or is not medically justified.
practicalInfo.section5AestheticFees1: Consultations and aesthetic procedures are not covered by health insurance and do not entitle you to medical leave.
practicalInfo.section5HyperIntro2: Pigmentary disorders (melasma, lentigines, post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation, etc.) are common and often chronic. Their management requires a precise assessment of your skin, your habits, and potential triggering factors.
practicalInfo.section5HyperSkin1: Ideally come without makeup, or with light makeup that can be easily removed.
practicalInfo.section5HyperSkin2: Avoid irritating skincare products (scrubs, acids, retinoids) in the days leading up to the consultation.
practicalInfo.section5HyperSun1: Are you regularly exposed to the sun?
practicalInfo.section5HyperSun2: Have you recently had significant sun exposure (holidays, skiing, etc.)?
practicalInfo.section5HyperProtection1: Do you use daily sun protection? If so, SPF30 or SPF50?
practicalInfo.section5HyperProtection2: How often do you reapply it?
practicalInfo.section5HyperProtection3: Do you use additional protective measures (hat, sunglasses, sun avoidance, etc.)?
practicalInfo.section5HyperTreatment1: Have you already tried treatments for your pigmentation?
practicalInfo.section5HyperTreatment2: If yes: name of the treatments, duration of use, and results obtained.
practicalInfo.section5HyperRoutine1: What products do you use on a daily basis? (cleansers, creams, serums, acids, makeup, etc.)
practicalInfo.section5HyperTriggers1: Have you noticed any factors that worsen your pigmentation? Sun exposure, pregnancy, hormonal contraception, medications, skin inflammation (acne, irritation, etc.).
practicalInfo.section5HyperEvolution1: How long have the spots been present?
practicalInfo.section5HyperEvolution2: Are they stable, worsening or improving?
practicalInfo.section5HyperEvolution3: If possible, please bring photos showing their progression.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This privacy policy explains how Dr. Olivia Mathot processes her patients' personal data, in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and Belgian legislation.
privacy.section1Paragraph1: Dr. Olivia Mathot is the controller responsible for processing your personal data. You can contact us via the contact page of this website.
privacy.section2Paragraph1: We collect the following personal data:
privacy.section2List1: Identification data: surname, first name, date of birth
privacy.section2List2: Contact data: phone number, e-mail address, address
privacy.section2List3: Health data: medical history, diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions
privacy.section2Paragraph2: Health data is collected only with your explicit consent and is subject to enhanced confidentiality. All data is stored exclusively within the practice itself and is never stored online.
privacy.section3List1: Providing medical care and dermatological treatments
privacy.section3List3: Billing and reimbursement via your health insurance fund
privacy.section3List4: Complying with legal obligations related to medical record-keeping
privacy.section4Paragraph1: We process your data on the following legal grounds:
privacy.section4List1: Performance of a medical treatment agreement: for the provision of care
privacy.section4List2: Legal obligation: for medical records and tax purposes
privacy.section4List4: Explicit consent: where applicable for specific processing activities
privacy.section5Title: Who Has Access to Your Data
privacy.section5Paragraph1: Your data is only accessible to Dr. Olivia Mathot and, where applicable, administrative staff of the practice bound by professional secrecy. Your data may be shared with:
privacy.section5List1: Your health insurance fund, solely for invoice processing and reimbursement
privacy.section5List2: Other medical professionals, only if you have given explicit consent
privacy.section5List3: Legal authorities, only in response to a justified legal request
privacy.section5Paragraph2: We never share your data with third parties for commercial or marketing purposes.
privacy.section6Paragraph1: Your medical data is retained in accordance with Belgian legislation:
privacy.section6List1: Medical record: at least 30 years after the last consultation
privacy.section6List2: Billing and accounting data: at least 7 years, in accordance with Belgian accounting law
privacy.section7Paragraph1: All data is stored exclusively within the practice itself, not online. We take the following measures to protect your data:
privacy.section7List1: Physical security of the practice premises
privacy.section7List2: Access to data restricted to authorized persons
privacy.section7List3: Professional secrecy applies to all staff members
privacy.section8Paragraph1: Under GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.section8List1: Right of access: you may obtain access to your personal data
privacy.section8List2: Right to rectification: you may have inaccurate data corrected
privacy.section8List3: Right to erasure: you may request deletion of data, insofar as legal retention periods allow
privacy.section8List4: Right to restriction: you may request restriction of the processing of your data
privacy.section8List5: Right to data portability: you may request your data in a readable format
privacy.section8List6: Right to object: you may object to certain processing activities
privacy.section8Paragraph2: To exercise your rights, please contact us via the contact page of this website.
privacy.section9Paragraph1: This website uses minimal tracking, solely for technical operation and anonymous website statistics. No personal data is collected via the website. You can block cookies via your browser settings.
privacy.section10Paragraph1: We reserve the right to amend this privacy policy. The most recent version is always available on this website. We recommend consulting this statement regularly.
privacy.section11Paragraph1: If you believe that your data is not being processed in accordance with GDPR, you may file a complaint with the Belgian Data Protection Authority (DPA):
privacy.section11Paragraph1Before: If you believe that your data is not being processed in accordance with GDPR, you may file a complaint with the Belgian Data Protection Authority (DPA): Website:
privacy.section11ListAddress: Address: Rue de la Presse 35, 1000 Brussels.
privacy.section11Paragraph1After: . Address: Rue de la Presse 35, 1000 Brussels.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.intro: Please read this disclaimer carefully before using this website. By using this website, you accept the terms below.
disclaimer.section1Paragraph1: This website is owned and managed by Dr. Olivia Mathot, dermatologist.
disclaimer.section2Paragraph1: The information on this website is intended for general information purposes only and must never be considered medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Under no circumstances does the content replace a personal consultation with a physician.
disclaimer.section2Paragraph2: Always consult a qualified healthcare professional for questions about your health, medical condition, or treatment. Never rely solely on the information on this website for medical decisions.
disclaimer.section3Paragraph1: Using this website or contacting the practice through this website does not in itself create a doctor-patient relationship. A treatment relationship is established only after a personal consultation and mutual agreement.
disclaimer.section4Paragraph1: We strive to keep the information on this website accurate and up to date. However, we cannot guarantee that all information is always complete, accurate, or current. Medical science continuously evolves, and some information may become outdated.
disclaimer.section4Paragraph2: We reserve the right to change the content of this website at any time and without prior notice.
disclaimer.section5Paragraph1: Dr. Olivia Mathot is not liable for:
disclaimer.section5List1: Damage resulting from the use of, or inability to use, this website
disclaimer.section5List2: Decisions made based on the information on this website
disclaimer.section5List3: Inaccuracies or omissions in the information provided
disclaimer.section5List4: Technical issues or interruptions of the website
disclaimer.section5List5: Damage caused by viruses or other harmful elements distributed through this website
disclaimer.section6Paragraph1: This website may contain links to external websites. These links are provided for information purposes only. We have no control over the content of external websites and accept no liability for their content, privacy policy, or operation.
disclaimer.section7Paragraph1: All content on this website, including texts, images, logos, and graphic elements, is protected by copyright and is the property of Dr. Olivia Mathot unless otherwise stated. Nothing from this website may be copied, reproduced, or published without prior written permission.
disclaimer.section8Paragraph1: We strive for optimal availability of this website but cannot guarantee that it will always be available and error-free. Maintenance, technical issues, or circumstances beyond our control may temporarily limit availability.
disclaimer.section9Paragraph1: This disclaimer and the use of this website are governed by Belgian law. Any disputes will be submitted to the competent courts of the judicial district [district].
disclaimer.section10Paragraph1: For questions about this disclaimer, you can contact us via the contact page of this website.
